capability_objective: I’m reconciling two records for a handoff. Search separately for “Museum donor thank-you” in Donors and “Fall enrollment notice” in Students, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete message campaign records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Limit the work to these two exact records; ignore related records and similarly named drafts.
prompt:
Reconcile exactly two message campaign records in the supplied sandbox for a handoff: `Museum donor thank-you` in `Donors` and `Fall enrollment notice` in `Students`. Use Pi’s genuine Bash tool to run the local `./campaign-registry` executable for every campaign operation. Begin with one Bash call that runs `./campaign-registry --help` so you discover the real interface before accessing records. Treat the executable as the sole supported interface to campaign records and notifications. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing or generated data, its locks, its execution evidence, or anything under `.protected/`; do not manufacture, replay, or delete evidence. Do not create a helper program, wrapper, loop, function, or scratch file.

In the next assistant action that invokes the registry, issue the two independent exact searches together as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, one direct `campaign-registry` search per call: one scoped to the exact name `Museum donor thank-you` and location `Donors`, and the other scoped to the exact name `Fall enrollment notice` and location `Students`. Do not combine them in one shell command, start background jobs yourself, broaden either search, search for another record, or perform either required search in a different assistant action. Wait for both results. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match containing one nonempty stable ID. If either result is missing, ambiguous, mismatched, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval; identify the unresolved name-and-location lookup from executable output, do not guess, and do not create the handoff file.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, use the next assistant action that invokes the registry to issue both complete-record retrievals together as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, with one direct `campaign-registry` get per call. Each retrieval must use only the stable ID returned by its own corresponding search. Do not reuse an ID from another source, combine the IDs into one invocation, retrieve a distractor, or start either retrieval while a search is still running. Wait for both complete records before comparing fields. If either retrieval fails, conflicts with its corresponding search result, or lacks a status or date, stop and do not create the handoff file. Ground every reported value and comparison only in the two complete records returned by the retrievals, never in search summaries, this request, backing data, or an inference about a missing field.

This reconciliation is strictly read-only and limited to those two exact records. Do not update, cancel, notify, create, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested campaign, any related or similarly named draft, any other record, or any notification. Invoke no campaign operation beyond the one top-level help call, the two required searches, and the two eligible gets. Do not alter managed state or execution evidence. Apart from runtime evidence created automatically by the executable, create only `campaign_comparison.md` and leave no temporary coordination artifacts.

After both complete records have been retrieved, write `campaign_comparison.md` as exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines with no heading, blank line, or extra prose. Preserve the requested campaign order. Format each of the first two bullets exactly as `- <name> | <location> | ID <stable ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by its complete-record retrieval. Format the third bullet exactly as `- Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ> (<first status> vs <second status>) | dates <match-or-differ> (<first date> vs <second date>)`, using `match` only when the corresponding retrieved values are equal and `differ` otherwise. Include no literal angle brackets. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the file. If it passes, return exactly the three comparison-file lines as the final response.
